prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Transformer T-015 shows rising load and intermittent over-temperature alerts. Investigate recent sensor behavior, infer probable fault mode, estimate short-term risk over 30 days, and issue a maintenance work order recommendation.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should show a multi-step workflow: summarize relevant IoT evidence, map to likely fault mode, provide a 30-day risk or forecast statement, and conclude with a concrete work-order recommendation.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [iot] list_assets — List all Smart Grid transformer assets with health status
  Step 2: [iot] get_asset_metadata — Get the metadata for transformer T-015 (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [iot] list_sensors — List all sensor IDs available for transformer T-015 (depends on steps [2])
  Step 4: [iot] get_sensor_readings — Get time-series readings for the winding_temp_top_c sensor on transformer T-015 (depends on steps [3])
  Step 5: [fmsr] list_failure_modes — List all failure modes
  Step 6: [fmsr] search_failure_modes — Search for failure modes related to over-temperature (depends on steps [5])
  Step 7: [fmsr] get_sensor_correlation — Get the sensors and gases most strongly associated with the probable fault mode (depends on steps [6])
  Step 8: [fmsr] get_dga_record — Get the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-015 (depends on steps [2])
  Step 9: [fmsr] analyze_dga — Classify a set of dissolved gas concentrations into a fault type using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method (depends on steps [8])
  Step 10: [tsfm] forecast_rul — Project the RUL forward by 30 days using a linear degradation model (depends on steps [2])
  Step 11: [tsfm] detect_anomalies — Detect anomalous sensor readings using a rolling z-score method (depends on steps [4])
  Step 12: [tsfm] trend_analysis — Compute the trend (slope) of the winding_temp_top_c sensor's readings over a time window (depends on steps [4])
  Step 13: [wo] estimate_downtime — Estimate the expected downtime (hours) for maintenance on transformer T-015 (depends on steps [2])
  Step 14: [wo] create_work_order — Create a new maintenance work order for transformer T-015 (depends on steps [13])
  Step 15: [iot] list_sensors — List all sensor IDs available for transformer T-015
  Step 16: [iot] get_sensor_readings — Get time-series readings for the winding_temp_top_c sensor on transformer T-015 (depends on steps [15])
  Step 17: [fmsr] list_failure_modes — List all failure modes
  Step 18: [fmsr] search_failure_modes — Search for failure modes related to over-temperature (depends on steps [17])
  Step 19: [fmsr] get_sensor_correlation — Get the sensors and gases most strongly associated with the probable fault mode (depends on steps [18])
  Step 20: [fmsr] get_dga_record — Get the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-015 (depends on steps [16])
  Step 21: [fmsr] analyze_dga — Classify a set of dissolved gas concentrations into a fault type using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method (depends on steps [20])
  Step 22: [tsfm] forecast_rul — Project the RUL forward by 30 days using a linear degradation model (depends on steps [21])
  Step 23: [tsfm] detect_anomalies — Detect anomalous sensor readings using a rolling z-score method (depends on steps [16])
  Step 24: [tsfm] trend_analysis — Compute the trend (slope) of the winding_temp_top_c sensor's readings over a time window (depends on steps [16])
  Step 25: [wo] estimate_downtime — Estimate the expected downtime (hours) for maintenance on transformer T-015 (depends on steps [22])
  Step 26: [wo] create_work_order — Create a new maintenance work order for transformer T-015 (depends on steps [25])
  Step 27: [tsfm] detect_anomalies — Detect anomalous sensor readings using a rolling z-score method
  Step 28: [tsfm] trend_analysis — Compute the trend (slope) of the winding_temp_top_c sensor's readings over a time window (depends on steps [27])
  Step 29: [wo] estimate_downtime — Estimate the expected downtime (hours) for maintenance on transformer T-015 (depends on steps [28])
  Step 30: [wo] create_work_order — Create a new maintenance work order for transformer T-015 (depends on steps [29])

## Agent Final Answer
Based on the results, the probable fault mode for transformer T-015 is a Thermal Fault 300-700°C, likely due to rising load and intermittent over-temperature alerts. The estimated short-term risk over 30 days is high, with a projected RUL of 24 hours. A maintenance work order has been issued with a priority of high and an estimated downtime of 24 hours. The recommended action is immediate de-energization and emergency inspection.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response: {"task_completion": true, "data_retrieval_accuracy": false, "generalized_result_verification": false, "agent_sequence_correct": false, "clarity_and_justification": false, "hallucinations": true, "suggestions": "Improve data retrieval accuracy and verify results against expected outputs"}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Improve data retrieval accuracy and verify results against expected outputs
